name: tsx (TypeScript Execute — Fast ts-node Alternative)

auth.notes: Local TypeScript execution tool; no auth

performance.notes: esbuild-based; ~5-10x faster startup than ts-node; no type checking

agent_readiness.notes: Drop-in ts-node replacement powered by esbuild. `tsx script.ts` or `tsx watch script.ts`. No tsconfig required. ESM + CJS support. --watch mode for development. node --import tsx/esm for ESM loader. node --require @tsx/cjs for CJS. Works with Node.js 12.22+. No type-checking (transpile-only like ts-node --transpileOnly). For scripts, CLIs, and development. TypeScript paths support. Faster than ts-node, simpler than ts-node/esm. Used in Vitest internals. Also: Bun and Deno natively execute TS without tsx.

gotchas: No type checking — transpile only (intentional for speed)
ESM/CJS interop needs correct loader flags
Bun and Deno make tsx unnecessary for those runtimes
best_when: Running TypeScript scripts without compilation step
Development watch mode for TypeScript projects
CI jobs needing fast TypeScript execution
Replacing ts-node for faster startup in tooling
avoid_when: Production deployments (compile with tsc or esbuild instead)
When type-checking at runtime is required
summary: tsx is a fast drop-in replacement for ts-node, powered by esbuild. `tsx script.ts` runs TypeScript files 5-10x faster than ts-node with no configuration needed. Supports ESM + CJS, --watch mode, and Node.js loader flags. No type-checking (transpile-only). Best for scripts, CLIs, and development tooling where TypeScript compilation speed matters. Bun/Deno users don't need tsx.
